sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports difficulty swallowing certain foods over the past several weeks, accompanied by chest pain when swallowing. He notes that the discomfort is more pronounced with larger food pieces. He reports experiencing some epigastric pain and occasional belly pain. He has gained approximately ten pounds over the past month. He reports increased stress related to a recent move and drinking more, including pizza and burgers.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 130/70 mmHg. Heart rate is 72 bpm. Oxygen saturation is 100%. Respiratory rate is 18 breaths per minute. Physical exam reveals no rash, lesions, or tenderness to palpation in the abdomen. Lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally. The barium swallow revealed two areas of mild esophageal narrowing in the mid and lower portions of the esophagus, consistent with esophagitis.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary problem is acute esophagitis. Weight gain is a secondary finding related to the patient's reported stress and dietary changes.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe protonix 40 mg daily. Prescribe carafate 1 gram four times daily for one month. Schedule an upper endoscopy in one week. Advise the patient to avoid citrus foods, fruits, and spicy foods until symptoms improve. Recommend eating slowly and chewing food thoroughly. Advise the patient to decrease alcohol and caffeine intake. Schedule a follow-up appointment next week for endoscopy review.
